task:
# Relay stage 3: Diagnose
Task: manually-mark-tasks-as-done
Working directory: /Users/nicholaswestby/Dev/visual-relay/

## Task input
# Manually Mark Tasks as Done

Add a per-task "Mark done" action that retires a non-archived task straight into the archive **without running it** — for tasks completed outside Visual Relay that were never moved out of the queue (e.g. `windows-support`). It reuses the existing retirement/archive machinery and reloads the list so the task leaves the queue immediately.

## Current state (researched)

- `RelayTaskItem` (`src/VisualRelay.Domain/RelayTaskItem.cs`) carries `IsArchived`, `MarkdownPath`, `TaskDirectory`, `IsNested`; `StateLabel` is `"Completed"` when archived, else `"Pending"`/`"Needs review"`.
- Retirement + archive is `TaskCompletionArchive.RetireAsync(rootPath, config, taskId, task)` (`src/VisualRelay.Core/Tasks/TaskCompletionArchive.cs`, `internal static`): renames `<id>.md` → `DONE-<id>.md` and, when `config.ArchiveOnDone`, moves it under `llm-tasks/completed/[batch-N/]`. Returns `RetirementResult` (`Additions`, `Rollback`, `DestinationPath`). Today only the driver calls it, in `RelayDriver.CommitGate.cs` `ExecuteCommitStageAsync`. It is `internal`, and VisualRelay.App has no `InternalsVisibleTo` into Core — so the App cannot call it directly.
- `RelayTaskRepository` (`src/VisualRelay.Core/Tasks/RelayTaskRepository.cs`, `public`, not `partial`) lists pending via `ListAsync`/`ListPendingAsync` (skips `DONE-`/`IGNORE-` names in `Walk`/`IsSkippedName`) and archived via `ListCompletedAsync`. File is 291 lines.
- The VM never git-commits authoring mutations: `SaveEditAsync`/`RenameAsync` (`MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs`) write files then `await ReloadTaskListAsync(...)`; only `RelayDriver` commits. `ReloadTaskListAsync` (`MainWindowViewModel.Helpers.cs`) re-scans and rebuilds `Tasks`. `RunBusyAsync` (same file) guards on `IsBusy` and surfaces faults to `StatusText`. `FormatQueueStatus()` is the queue-status string.
- Per-selected-task action buttons live in the `TaskDetailPanel` header (`src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml`): a `StackPanel` with `Classes="primary"` `Run selected` / `Resume`. That file is **exactly 300 lines** — the guard is `FileSizeGuard.cs` `if (lines > limit)` with `DefaultLimit = 300`, so no inline addition fits.
- `MainWindowViewModel.Commands.cs` is 298 lines — near the ceiling; new commands go in a partial.
- Confirmations use `ShowConfirmationAsync` (`MainWindowViewModel.cs`: `Func<string,string,string,Task<bool>>?`), wired in `App.axaml.cs`; `null` in tests/headless skips the prompt (see `MainWindowViewModel.Rewrite.cs` `RewriteSelectedTaskAsync`). Can-execute pattern: `CanRunSelected()` = `!IsBusy && !PauseRequested && SelectedTask is not null && !SelectedTask.IsArchived && !_rewritingTaskIds.Contains(SelectedTask.Id)`.
- Control API: `ControlApi.ResolveCommand` (`src/VisualRelay.App/Services/ControlApi.cs`) maps names → commands; `IcommandNames` in `ControlApi.State.cs` (~line 97) lists them for `/state`. Disabled commands return 409.
- Tests: `TestRepository.Create()`/`WriteConfig(testCommand, logSources, archiveOnDone: true)`/`WriteTask`/`WriteNestedTask` (`tests/VisualRelay.Tests/TestDoubles.cs`); headless UI tests use `[AvaloniaFact]` + `window.GetVisualDescendants()` (e.g. `TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs`).

## What to build (TDD-first)

1. **Core retire wrapper.** Add `public Task<string?> MarkDoneAsync(RelayTaskItem task, CancellationToken ct = default)` to `RelayTaskRepository`: load config (`RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync`), return `null` if not `Loaded`, else call `TaskCompletionArchive.RetireAsync(RootPath, config, task.Id, task)` and return `retirement?.DestinationPath`. **No git commit** — matches create/edit/rename. Keep `RelayTaskRepository.cs` ≤ 300 (it is 291); if it would exceed, mark the class `partial` and move the method into `RelayTaskRepository.MarkDone.cs`.
   - Test (mirror `TaskCompletionArchiveNoBatchTests.cs`): with `archiveOnDone:true`, `MarkDoneAsync` on a pending task moves `llm-tasks/<id>/<id>.md` under `completed/`; afterward `ListPendingAsync` excludes it and `ListCompletedAsync` includes it (`IsArchived`, `StateLabel == "Completed"`).

2. **VM command.** In a **new partial** `MainWindowViewModel.MarkDone.cs`, add `[RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanMarkSelectedTaskDone))] async Task MarkSelectedTaskDoneAsync()`: guard on `SelectedTask`; confirm via `ShowConfirmationAsync` (skip when `null`) — title "Mark task done", message ``Move "<id>" to the archive? It won't be run by Visual Relay.``, confirm "Mark done"; on confirm, `await new RelayTaskRepository(RootPath).MarkDoneAsync(SelectedTask.Task)`, then `await ReloadTaskListAsync()` and `StatusText = FormatQueueStatus()`, all inside `RunBusyAsync`. `CanMarkSelectedTaskDone()` = `SelectedTask is not null && !SelectedTask.IsArchived && !ShowArchive && !IsBusy && !_runningTaskIds.Contains(SelectedTask.Id)`. Add `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand))]` to `_selectedTask`, `_showArchive`, and `_isBusy`, and re-notify it when running-task set changes (mirroring how `RunSelectedCommand` is refreshed).
   - Test (headless, `ShowConfirmationAsync = null`): mark-done on a pending task removes it from `viewModel.Tasks`; `CanMarkSelectedTaskDone` is false for an archived task, while `ShowArchive`, while `IsBusy`, and for a running task.

3. **Button — extract to stay under 300.** Extract the `TaskDetailPanel` header action `StackPanel` (Run selected / Resume) into a new child control `Views/Controls/TaskActionBar.axaml`(+`.axaml.cs`), `x:DataType="vm:MainWindowViewModel"`, referenced from `TaskDetailPanel.axaml` as `<controls:TaskActionBar/>` (net line reduction keeps `TaskDetailPanel.axaml` ≤ 300). Add a **plain (non-primary)** `Mark done` button there with `ToolTip.Tip="Move this task to the archive without running it."`, `Command="{Binding MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand}"`, and `IsVisible` bound to a new computed `IsMarkDoneButtonVisible` (`SelectedTask is not null && !SelectedTask.IsArchived && !ShowArchive`, notified from the same sources). Placement next to Run/Resume keeps it with the other selected-task actions; plain styling makes it visually subordinate to the primary run actions; hidden for archived tasks / the archive view.
   - Test (`[AvaloniaFact]`, `TaskDetailRemoveButtonLayoutTests.cs` style): with a non-archived task selected, a `Button` whose content is "Mark done" is visible and bound to `MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand`; it is hidden when an archived task is selected.

4. **Control API.** In `ControlApi.ResolveCommand` add `"mark-done" => viewModel.MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand`; add `"mark-done"` to `IcommandNames` in `ControlApi.State.cs`. Flow is `select-task` then `mark-done` (operates on the selected task); disabled → 409.

## Done when

- Selecting a pending task and clicking "Mark done" (confirming) moves its file under `llm-tasks/completed/` and it disappears from the queue; toggling the archive view shows it as completed.
- The button is hidden for archived tasks and in the archive view, disabled while busy or for a running task, and always asks for confirmation in the live app (skipped headless).
- `POST /command/mark-done` retires the selected task; `/state` lists `mark-done` with the correct `enabled` flag.
- `./visual-relay check` passes; every changed `.cs`/`.axaml` is ≤ 300 lines; headless UI tests use `[AvaloniaFact]`; no per-machine state written in-repo.
- Conventional Commit subject, e.g. `feat: mark tasks done from the task panel`.


## Manifest
(not set yet)

## Prior stages
## Stage 1 - Ideate

{
  "summary": "Add a \"Mark done\" button/command that retires a selected pending task into the archive without running it, reusing TaskCompletionArchive.RetireAsync. Key challenge: TaskDetailPanel.axaml and RelayTaskRepository.cs are both at the 300-line SplitGuard ceiling, so additions need extraction or partial-class splitting. Also RetireAsync is internal — needs a public wrapper (or InternalsVisibleTo).",
  "options": [
    "Option A (spec-following): Add MarkDoneAsync to RelayTaskRepository (making it partial if over 300), create MainWindowViewModel.MarkDone.cs partial, extract TaskActionBar.axaml child control from TaskDetailPanel.axaml, wire ControlApi \"mark-done\". Most file churn but cleanest separation.",
    "Option B (dedicated service): Create a new public TaskMarkDoneService in Core wrapping RetireAsync — no touch to RelayTaskRepository. VM command, TaskActionBar extraction, and Control API same as Option A. Fewer Core changes, single-responsibility service.",
    "Option C (minimal delta): Add InternalsVisibleTo so VM calls RetireAsync directly. Split TaskDetailPanel.axaml into two composed files to squeeze the button inline without extracting a new control. Fewest abstractions but weaker assembly boundary and awkward AXAML split."
  ]
}

## Stage 2 - Research

{
  "findings": "The codebase has a well-established pattern for partial classes (18 partial files for MainWindowViewModel). RelayTaskRepository (291 lines) and TaskDetailPanel.axaml (300 lines) both sit at/near the 300-line FileSizeGuard ceiling. Contrary to the task's stated assumption, VisualRelay.Core.csproj DOES have <InternalsVisibleTo Include=\"VisualRelay.App\" /> (line 27), so App can call internal Core members like TaskCompletionArchive.RetireAsync — but the public MarkDoneAsync wrapper on RelayTaskRepository is still the cleaner approach (no git commit, matches create/edit/rename pattern). ShowConfirmationAsync is a Func<string,string,string,Task<bool>>? property on MainWindowViewModel, wired in App.axaml.cs; null in headless tests skips the prompt. CanExecute patterns use [NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(...)] attributes on [ObservableProperty] fields. The RewriteToolbar.axaml control (UserControl with x:DataType=\"vm:MainWindowViewModel\", simple InitializeComponent code-behind) is the exact template for extracting TaskActionBar.axaml. ControlApi.ResolveCommand uses a switch expression; IcommandNames is a static string[] in ControlApi.State.cs. TestRepository in TestDoubles.cs provides WriteConfig/WriteTask/WriteNestedTask with deterministic temp paths. Headless UI tests use [AvaloniaFact] with window.GetVisualDescendants().",
  "constraints": [
    "Every .cs and .axaml file must be ≤ 300 lines (FileSizeGuard.DefaultLimit). RelayTaskRepository is 291 lines → MarkDoneAsync must go in a new RelayTaskRepository.MarkDone.cs partial or the class must be marked partial. TaskDetailPanel.axaml is exactly 300 lines → the header action StackPanel must be extracted into a child control (TaskActionBar.axaml). MainWindowViewModel.Commands.cs is 298 lines → new command goes in a new partial (MainWindowViewModel.MarkDone.cs).",
    "No git commit in MarkDoneAsync — retirement must write files to disk only, matching the save/create/rename pattern in Authoring.cs.",
    "Confirmation dialog: use ShowConfirmationAsync with null-guard (skip when null, i.e. headless). Title \"Mark task done\", message \"Move \"<id>\" to the archive? It won't be run by Visual Relay.\", confirm label \"Mark done\".",
    "Can-mark-done gating: CanMarkSelectedTaskDone() = SelectedTask is not null && !SelectedTask.IsArchived && !ShowArchive && !IsBusy && !_runningTaskIds.Contains(SelectedTask.Id). Notify via [NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand))] on _selectedTask, _showArchive, _isBusy; also re-notify when running-task set changes (same places RunSelectedCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged is called, e.g. in RaiseRewriteStateChanged and BeginRunningTask/ClearRunningTask).",
    "The Mark done button must be hidden (IsVisible bound to computed IsMarkDoneButtonVisible) for archived tasks and in the archive view. Placement as a plain (non-primary) button next to Run/Resume in the extracted TaskActionBar.",
    "Control API: add \"mark-done\" → viewModel.MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand to ResolveCommand switch, and \"mark-done\" to IcommandNames array. Disabled → returns 409.",
    "Headless UI tests use [AvaloniaFact] (Collection(\"Headless\")), locate controls via window.GetVisualDescendants().OfType<Button>().Where(b => b.Content?.ToString() == \"Mark done\"). Assert IsVisible and Command binding.",
    "Tests must use TestRepository.Create()/WriteConfig/WriteTask/WriteNestedTask for temp directories; ShowConfirmationAsync = null to skip headless prompt.",
    "Conventional commit: feat: mark tasks done from the task panel"
  ]
}



End your reply with a single fenced ```json block, nothing after it, matching: { "evidence": string, "excerpts": string[], "repro": string }

result.answer:
Here is the diagnosis:

```json
{
  "evidence": "The 'Mark done' feature has NOT been implemented. No MarkDone.* or TaskActionBar.* files exist anywhere in the repo. Five key files sit at or within 9 lines of the 300-line FileSizeGuard ceiling, forcing structural refactors before any new code can land:\n\n1. TaskDetailPanel.axaml: EXACTLY 300 lines — the header action StackPanel (lines 46-51 with Run/Resume buttons) MUST be extracted into a new child control (TaskActionBar.axaml) before adding a 'Mark done' button, because no inline addition fits.\n\n2. MainWindowViewModel.cs: EXACTLY 300 lines — can't add new observable properties or [NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor] attributes inline. New command + computed properties go in a new partial.\n\n3. MainWindowViewModel.Commands.cs: 298 lines (2-line margin) — new [RelayCommand] must go in a new partial.\n\n4. MainWindowViewModel.Helpers.cs: 299 lines (1-line margin) — effectively full.\n\n5. RelayTaskRepository.cs: 291 lines (9-line margin), but the class is 'public sealed class' (line 7), NOT 'partial'. To add MarkDoneAsync in a separate file, the class declaration must change to 'public sealed partial class'. 9 lines is too tight for the method body anyway (config load + RetireAsync call + null checks = ~20 lines).\n\nStructural prerequisites before implementation:\n- RelayTaskRepository.cs line 7: 'sealed class' → 'sealed partial class'\n- Extract TaskDetailPanel.axaml lines 20-52 (header StackPanel) into Views/Controls/TaskActionBar.axaml (+ .axaml.cs)\n- Create MainWindowViewModel.MarkDone.cs partial (following the 18-existing-partials pattern)\n- Create RelayTaskRepository.MarkDone.cs partial (following MainWindowViewModel's partial pattern)\n\nConfirmed enablers: InternalsVisibleTo from Core to App exists (Core.csproj line 27), ShowConfirmationAsync wired in App.axaml.cs, ControlApi.ResolveCommand (181 lines, room to grow), ControlApi.State.cs IcommandNames (103 lines, room to grow). Test patterns: [AvaloniaFact] + [Collection(\"Headless\")] + TestRepository.Create()/WriteConfig/WriteNestedTask from TestDoubles.cs (244 lines).",
  "excerpts": [
    "TaskDetailPanel.axaml:300 — header action StackPanel at lines 20-52 with Run/Resume buttons (lines 46-51) must be extracted; file is exactly at the 300-line ceiling",
    "RelayTaskRepository.cs:7 — 'public sealed class RelayTaskRepository(string rootPath, IGitInvoker? gitInvoker = null)' — sealed, NOT partial; must become 'sealed partial class'",
    "MainWindowViewModel.Commands.cs:298 — 2-line margin; new [RelayCommand] MarkSelectedTaskDoneAsync must go in new MainWindowViewModel.MarkDone.cs partial",
    "MainWindowViewModel.cs:82-83,104-107,138-140,193-198 — [NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor] attributes on _pauseRequested, _selectedTask, _showArchive, _isBusy must also include nameof(MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand)",
    "MainWindowViewModel.Rewrite.cs:31-39 — ShowConfirmationAsync pattern: guard on null (headless skip), await ShowConfirmationAsync(title, message, confirmLabel)",
    "MainWindowViewModel.Rewrite.cs:274-276 — RaiseRewriteStateChanged calls RunSelectedCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged() and ResumeSelectedCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged(); MarkSelectedTaskDoneCommand needs same treatment",
    "ControlApi.cs:28-45 — ResolveCommand switch expression has no 'mark-done' case; ControlApi.State.cs:97-101 — IcommandNames array missing 'mark-done'",
    "Core.csproj:27 — <InternalsVisibleTo Include=\"VisualRelay.App\" /> confirms App can call internal TaskCompletionArchive.RetireAsync directly",
    "VisualRelay.Core/Tasks/TaskCompletionArchive.cs:6 — 'internal static partial class TaskCompletionArchive' with public static RetirementResult? RetireAsync(rootPath, config, taskId, task) — no git commit in this method",
    "RelayTaskItem.cs:3-15 — record with IsArchived, MarkdownPath, TaskDirectory, IsNested; StateLabel => IsArchived ? 'Completed' : NeedsReview ? 'Needs review' : 'Pending'"
  ],
  "repro": "Run 'find . -name \"*MarkDone*\" -o -name \"*TaskActionBar*\"' — returns empty. Then 'sed -n \"$=\" src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml' → 300 (at ceiling). 'sed -n \"$=\" src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Commands.cs' → 298 (2-line margin). 'grep \"sealed class RelayTaskRepository\" src/VisualRelay.Core/Tasks/RelayTaskRepository.cs' → 'public sealed class' (not partial). These three constraints together mean no implementation has been attempted because every target file is structurally blocked from inline addition."
}
```
